meanings.haab.month.pop: Pop, the woven mat of authority, opens the Haab' year with leadership, community order and a fresh mandate to begin.
meanings.haab.month.wo: Wo' gathers the dark clouds before the rains, a time of quiet preparation, ritual patience and hidden gathering of strength.
meanings.haab.month.sip: Sip belongs to the hunt and the lords of the forest, stirring instinct, alertness and the pursuit of what you need.
meanings.haab.month.sotz: Sotz', the bat of the cave, sees in darkness — favouring intuition, night wisdom and trust in the unseen.
meanings.haab.month.sek: Sek balances sky and earth, a settled, fertile interval for tending bees, sweetness and the steady increase of abundance.
meanings.haab.month.xul: Xul, the dog who guides souls, marks endings and transitions, lighting the way through the close of a cycle.
meanings.haab.month.yaxkin: Yaxk'in, the new sun, brings warmth, ripening and clarity — a bright season for drying, finishing and bringing things to fruition.
meanings.haab.month.mol: Mol is the gathering and binding of waters and jade, calling for collection, communal effort and patient consolidation.
meanings.haab.month.chen: Ch'en, the well and the moon, draws on hidden waters, mystery and the deep inner sources of feeling and dream.
meanings.haab.month.yax: Yax, first and green, renews all things — the fresh blue-green of beginnings, growth and the Venus star.
meanings.haab.month.sak: Sak, the white, brings dawn light and purity, clearing the white road for honesty, recovery and fresh air.
meanings.haab.month.keh: Keh, the red deer, carries vigour, courage and the wild forest's vitality, urging swift, spirited movement.
meanings.haab.month.mak: Mak, the enclosure and lid, covers and protects — a sheltered, inward time for guarding what must not be disturbed.
meanings.haab.month.kankin: K'ank'in ripens to golden yellow, the late harvest's maturity tempered by the underworld dog's sober reminder of limits.
meanings.haab.month.muwan: Muwan, the screech-owl of the rain clouds, speaks of prophecy, cacao and the gathering storm — heed omens and signs.
meanings.haab.month.pax: Pax sounds the planting drum, music for sowing and the jaguar's strength, rallying courage for the great undertaking.
meanings.haab.month.kayab: K'ayab, the turtle, sings the rain-song of fertility and renewal, marking the turning of the year toward new water.
meanings.haab.month.kumku: Kumk'u, the granary of the old crocodile-earth, holds accumulated abundance — the harvest stored, wisdom of age, the year nearly full.
meanings.haab.month.wayeb: Wayeb', the five nameless days, is a liminal threshold of caution and rest; withdraw, keep still and prepare for renewal.
